zh.Calverhall, Shropshire, England: 英國史洛普爾卡爾福哈爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當John Le Barker於1436年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾哈隆時，他的父親Sir William Le Barker39歲，母親Lady Ann Colynson Rowlowe32歲。他於1450年與Elizabeth Greene在英國史洛普爾可拉維利結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1507年在英國英格蘭史洛普爾卡爾福哈爾享年71歲，埋葬在英國史洛普爾可拉維利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Le Barker于1436年出生在英国英格兰史洛普爾哈隆时，他的父亲Sir William Le Barker39岁，母亲Lady Ann Colynson Rowlowe32岁。他于1450年与Elizabeth Greene在英国史洛普爾可拉維利结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1507年在英国英格兰史洛普爾卡爾福哈爾享年71岁，埋葬在英国史洛普爾可拉維利。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Barker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a tanner of leather, from Middle English barkere ‘tanner’, tree bark having been used as the tanning agent.

English: occupational name for a shepherd, from Middle English berker, bercher (Old French berchier, bercher, berkier, berker, Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’, genitive berbicis). With the change of -ar- to -er- in Middle English, this became indistinguishable from the preceding name (see 1 above).
Americanized form of German   Berger   or   Barger  .

variants.name: Berger
meanings: German, Dutch, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived in the mountains or hills (see   Berg  ). The surname of German origin is also found in many other European countries, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Russia, Poland, Czechia, Hungary, and Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic topographic names or surnames. As a Jewish name it is mainly artificial. Compare   Bargar  ,   Barger  ,   Barker  ,   Barrier  , and   Barriger  .
French: occupational name from Old French bergier ‘shepherd’ (from Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’). It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin. Compare Shepard .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farms so named with the plural of   Berg   ‘mountain’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When John Le Barker was born in 1436, in Hallon, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir William Le Barker, was 39 and his mother, Lady Ann Colynson Rowlowe, was 32. He married Elizabeth Greene in 1450, in Claverley, Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters. He died in 1507, in Calverhall, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 71, and was buried in Claverley, Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Le Barker est né en 1436, à Hallon, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir William Le Barker, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Lady Ann Colynson Rowlowe, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Greene en 1450, à Claverley, Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé en 1507, à Calverhall, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterré à Claverley, Shropshire, Angleterre.

de.Holland, Lincolnshire, England: Parts of Holland, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als John Le Barker 1436 in Hallon, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William Le Barker 39 und seine Mutter Lady Ann Colynson Rowlowe 32. Er hat 1450 in Claverley, Shropshire, England Elizabeth Greene geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb 1507 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Calverhall, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Claverley, Shropshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Le Barker è nato il nel 1436 nel Hallon, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir William Le Barker, aveva 39 anni e il suo madre, Lady Ann Colynson Rowlowe, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Greene nel 1450, nel Claverley, Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie. Morì nel 1507, nel Calverhall, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 71 anni, e fu sepolto a Claverley, Shropshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: John Le Barkerが1436年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアハロンで生まれた時，父Sir William Le Barkerは39歳，母Lady Ann Colynson Rowloweは32歳であった。1450年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアクラバーリーでElizabeth Greeneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1507年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアカルバーハルで71歳で死去し，イギリスシュロプシュアイアクラバーリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Holland, Lincolnshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 파르츠 프 홀랜드
ko.lifeSummary: John Le Barker(이)가 1436년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 핼런에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Le Barker(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Ann Colynson Rowlowe(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1450년 영국 슈럽실르 클레이벌리에서 Elizabeth Greene(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1507년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 캘버홀에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르 클레이벌리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Le Barker nasceu em 1436, em Hallon, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir William Le Barker, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Lady Ann Colynson Rowlowe, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Greene em 1450, em Claverley, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1507, em Calverhall, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 71 anos, e foi sepultado em Claverley, Shropshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Holland, Lincolnshire, England: парц оф холланд, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Le Barker родился в 1436 г. в местности холлон, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir William Le Barker было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Ann Colynson Rowlowe, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Greene в 1450 г. в местности клавирли, шропшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей. Он умер в 1507 г. в местности калвиролл, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 71 год и был похоронен в местности клавирли, шропшир, Англия.

es.Holland, Lincolnshire, England: Parts of Holland, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Le Barker nació en 1436, en Hallon, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir William Le Barker, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Lady Ann Colynson Rowlowe, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Greene en 1450, en Claverley, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió en 1507, en Calverhall, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultado en Claverley, Shropshire, Inglaterra.
